name: Dolphin Entertainment, Inc.
business: Dolphin Entertainment provides strategic marketing and publicity services to top brands in motion picture, television, music, gaming, culinary, hospitality, lifestyle, and charitable industries through subsidiaries 42West, The Door, Shore Fire, The Digital Dept., Elle, and Special Projects. The company also produces feature films and digital content through Dolphin Films. It was recognized as the #1 PR firm in the country in the Observer rankings in 2025.

exposures.sentence: Video Game Publicity: We provide marketing direction, public relations counsel and media strategy for video game publishers and other entities in the video gaming industry.

Entertainment Consumer Product Marketing: We provide marketing direction, public relations counsel and media strategy for consumer product companies and divisions of major entertainment studios, and entertainment memorabilia companies.
We rely on information technology systems that are susceptible to cybersecurity risks.
Our ability to adapt to rapid change in technology (including artificial intelligence), forms of delivery, storage, and consumer preferences related to digital content

catalysts.event: Partnered with Aircraft Productions to produce Youngblood reboot
catalysts.sentence: In February, 2025, Dolphin Films partnered with Aircraft Productions of Toronto, Canada to produce a re-boot of the popular 1986 MGM hockey movie Youngblood.

catalysts.event: Entered into distribution agreement with Well Go USA for Youngblood
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we entered into a distribution agreement with Well Go USA, Inc. ( Well Go ) to distribute the film across all media in the United States.
The film premiered in theaters on March 6, 2026.
On November 14, 2025 (the AA Closing Date ), we sold all of the membership interests in Always Alpha Sports Management LLC ( Always Alpha ) to Always Alpha Holdings, LLC
